gene_region.label: NR1D1 transcribed interval

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: NR1D1 encodes REV-ERB alpha, a heme-sensitive nuclear receptor that links circadian timing to lipid metabolism and immunity. This topic bundle emphasizes obscure high-impact biology, with interpretation centered on rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local NR1D1 bundle is oriented toward obscure high-impact biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: NR1D1 variants can alter, tag, or contextualize rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.

Rare disease, trait modifier, sensory, metabolic, immune, digestive, and neurobehavioral pathway review

gene_context.methylation_interpretation: NR1D1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for NR1D1.
Because NR1D1 biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against rare-variant mechanisms, unexpected pathway crosstalk, sensory perception, mitochondrial stress, neuroimmune signaling, epithelial defense, and metabolic edge cases.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use NR1D1 promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
